mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive direction in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5%, it signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering a recessionary pricing regime that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) during periods of structural labor shortages.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real personal spending and moderating inflation indicates healthy demand without price pressures, creating a soft-landing environment that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS growth exceeds CPIAUCSL growth by less than 0.5%, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals expanding corporate output capacity and improving profit margins, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days specifically during early-cycle expansion phases.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in >55% of early-cycle observations, or the predictive accuracy drops below random chance when industrial capacity utilization exceeds 85%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust economic activity and rising corporate revenues, consistently generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth correlates negatively with 20-day forward returns in >60% of monthly observations, or the signal Rank IC falls below 0.01 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year housing starts decline exceeding 5% signals a sharp contraction in construction activity and consumer confidence, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines by more than 5% year-over-year
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >65% of periods following a >5% YoY housing starts drop, or the threshold effect vanishes when mortgage rates fall below 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining industrial production and rising unemployment indicates a broad-based economic slowdown, creating a negative earnings revision cycle that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to predict negative returns when the divergence persists for fewer than 3 months, or the combined signal shows positive predictive accuracy in >50% of test cases.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5% confirms strong aggregate demand and corporate earnings momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days during mid-cycle expansion phases.
failure_condition: The signal generates negative forward returns in >60% of quarters where GDPC1 growth surpasses 2.5%, or the predictive relationship reverses during periods of supply chain bottlenecks.
